title: B1. USACO 2024 US Open Platinum / Identity Theft
label: mx1_review_03
task_text:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: coding-B1-usaco-2024-us-open-platinum-identity-theft
Title: B1. USACO 2024 US Open Platinum / Identity Theft

Problem:
Extend cows' bitstring IDs so no cow can be mistaken for another when some reported IDs are truncated.

Grading target:
Binary hidden-test acceptance.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>
root_result_excerpt:
FINAL_ANSWER: The minimum length $L$ is the maximum depth of a Trie constructed from the given bitstring IDs, where each original ID is extended to become a leaf node (ensuring no ID is a prefix of another).

JUSTIFICATION: To ensure no cow's ID is a prefix of another (which would cause ambiguity upon truncation), the set of IDs must form a prefix-free code. The most efficient way to achieve this
final_result_excerpt:
FINAL_ANSWER: The minimum length $L$ is the maximum depth of a Trie constructed from the given bitstring IDs, where each original ID is extended to become a leaf node (ensuring no ID is a prefix of another).

assessment.summary: This is the first scored attempt in the loop history.

what_helped: The loop preserved the question and family boundary so changes stay interpretable.
The current prompt is isolated as the only variable being optimized.

recommendation.reason: The answers are still wrong, so this phase should keep exploring meaningfully different prompt directions.
